question: A project manager is halfway through a 24-month infrastructure upgrade project. A senior engineer who holds most of the architectural knowledge resigns unexpectedly. The PM realizes that if another key team member leaves, the project will be at serious risk. What should the PM do FIRST to address this situation?
options: Document the architectural decisions and rationale made so far in the lessons learned register before the engineer departs
Immediately escalate to the sponsor and request a project pause until a replacement is hired
Update the risk register to reflect the increased bus-factor risk and monitor it monthly
Conduct a knowledge transfer session where the departing engineer trains the remaining team
explanation: The most immediate and effective action is a structured knowledge transfer session before the engineer leaves, capturing both explicit and tacit knowledge. While documenting in the lessons learned register is also important, live knowledge transfer from the departing person captures experience that is hard to document alone. Escalating for a pause is premature, and simply updating the risk register without action does not mitigate the risk.
explanation_vi: Hành động tức thời và hiệu quả nhất là tổ chức buổi chuyển giao kiến thức có cấu trúc trước khi kỹ sư rời đi, thu thập cả kiến thức hiện và ẩn. Mặc dù ghi vào sổ bài học kinh nghiệm cũng quan trọng, nhưng chuyển giao kiến thức trực tiếp từ người sắp rời đi giúp nắm bắt kinh nghiệm khó ghi chép. Leo thang để tạm dừng là quá sớm, và chỉ cập nhật sổ rủi ro mà không hành động không làm giảm rủi ro.

question: During a sprint retrospective, the team identifies that a particular integration pattern saved them two days of rework. The scrum master wants to make sure this insight benefits future sprints and other teams. What is the BEST way to capture and share this knowledge?
options: Add the insight to the team's wiki or knowledge repository so it is accessible organization-wide
Mention it verbally during the next all-hands meeting and move on
Wait until project closure to compile all retrospective findings into a final lessons learned report
Email the insight only to the current team members so they can apply it next sprint
explanation: PMI emphasizes that lessons learned should be captured continuously throughout the project—not just at the end—and stored in a shared knowledge repository so other teams and future projects can benefit. Verbal mentions at meetings are not durable. Waiting until closure delays value. Emailing only the current team limits knowledge dissemination.
explanation_vi: PMI nhấn mạnh rằng bài học kinh nghiệm cần được thu thập liên tục trong suốt dự án—không chỉ ở cuối—và lưu trữ trong kho kiến thức chung để các nhóm khác và dự án tương lai có thể hưởng lợi. Đề cập bằng lời không bền vững. Chờ đến khi đóng cửa làm chậm giá trị. Gửi email chỉ cho nhóm hiện tại hạn chế việc phổ biến kiến thức.

question: A project is nearing completion and the PM is preparing to hand over a newly developed software system to the operations team. The operations team lead complains that the documentation provided is too technical and they won't be able to maintain the system without help. What should the PM do?
options: Tell the operations team lead to send their staff for technical training at their own cost
Work with the team to revise the handoff documentation to match the operations team's skill level and include runbooks and troubleshooting guides
Deliver the system as-is since the documentation meets the technical acceptance criteria agreed in the plan
Schedule a one-time walkthrough meeting and consider the handover complete
explanation: Effective knowledge transfer means ensuring the receiving team can actually operate what was delivered. Documentation quality for operations handover is a PMI best practice—deliverables should include runbooks, troubleshooting guides, and materials tailored to the operations team's needs. Delivering as-is ignores the spirit of transition planning. A single walkthrough without adequate documentation is insufficient.
explanation_vi: Chuyển giao kiến thức hiệu quả có nghĩa là đảm bảo nhóm tiếp nhận thực sự có thể vận hành những gì đã được bàn giao. Chất lượng tài liệu cho bàn giao vận hành là thực hành tốt nhất của PMI—các sản phẩm bàn giao cần bao gồm sổ hướng dẫn vận hành, hướng dẫn xử lý sự cố và tài liệu phù hợp với nhu cầu của nhóm vận hành.

question: A hybrid project team uses two-week sprints for development but follows a traditional phase-gate structure for overall governance. At the end of Phase 1, the steering committee asks for lessons learned. The PM notices the team has been holding retrospectives but never formally compiled the findings. What should the PM do?
options: Inform the committee that lessons learned are embedded in retrospective notes and no formal document is needed
Compile the retrospective outputs into a formal lessons learned register entry for the phase-gate review and update the knowledge repository
Reschedule the phase-gate review to allow time to conduct a proper lessons learned session from scratch
Ask team members to individually submit their lessons learned by email for the PM to collate
explanation: In a hybrid environment, retrospective outputs are valuable raw material that should be synthesized into the formal lessons learned register to satisfy governance requirements. The PM should compile existing retrospective findings rather than starting from scratch or dismissing the governance need. Individual email submissions are less systematic and harder to manage.
explanation_vi: Trong môi trường kết hợp, kết quả hồi cố là nguyên liệu thô có giá trị cần được tổng hợp vào sổ bài học kinh nghiệm chính thức để đáp ứng yêu cầu quản trị. PM nên tổng hợp các kết quả hồi cố hiện có thay vì bắt đầu từ đầu hoặc bỏ qua yêu cầu quản trị.

question: An agile team is being restructured mid-project, with three experienced members moving to a higher-priority initiative. The incoming replacement members have no context on decisions made during the first six months. The PM wants to ensure continuity. What is the MOST effective approach?
options: Have the departing members write detailed handoff packages covering decisions, patterns, and rationale before they transition
Assign the project sponsor to mentor the new members until they get up to speed
Restart the project planning process so the new members can build context from the beginning
Rely on the existing backlog and sprint board as sufficient context for the new members
explanation: Handoff packages that capture decisions, rationale, and patterns are the PMI-recommended approach to preserve continuity when team members change. Tacit knowledge—why certain decisions were made—is especially at risk when experienced members leave. The backlog and sprint board alone do not capture decision rationale. Restarting planning wastes time and sponsor mentorship is not a sustainable solution.
explanation_vi: Các gói bàn giao ghi lại các quyết định, lý do và mô hình là cách tiếp cận được PMI khuyến nghị để duy trì tính liên tục khi thành viên nhóm thay đổi. Kiến thức ẩn—lý do tại sao một số quyết định được đưa ra—đặc biệt có nguy cơ khi các thành viên có kinh nghiệm rời đi.

question: A project manager working on a complex ERP implementation notices that lessons learned sessions are only scheduled at project closure. Several near-miss events have occurred during execution that contain valuable information for the current project. What should the PM recommend?
options: Continue with the plan since the final lessons learned session will capture all incidents comprehensively
Establish periodic lessons learned capture sessions throughout the project so insights are available in real time
Document near-misses in the issue log and address them during the final lessons learned meeting
Assign a team member to informally track near-misses in a personal notebook
explanation: PMI's PMBOK emphasizes that lessons learned should be captured throughout the project lifecycle, not only at closure. Periodic sessions allow teams to apply insights immediately, improving the current project. Waiting until closure means valuable real-time learning is lost. The issue log alone does not fulfill the lessons learned requirement, and informal personal tracking is not a reliable knowledge management practice.
explanation_vi: PMBOK của PMI nhấn mạnh rằng bài học kinh nghiệm cần được thu thập trong suốt vòng đời dự án, không chỉ khi đóng cửa. Các buổi định kỳ cho phép nhóm áp dụng ngay những hiểu biết, cải thiện dự án hiện tại. Chờ đến khi đóng cửa có nghĩa là mất đi những bài học thời gian thực có giá trị.

question: A project is transitioning a new manufacturing process to the operations team. The PM has prepared comprehensive written documentation, but the operations manager notes that the nuances of operating the equipment safely come from experience that is difficult to write down. What technique should the PM prioritize to transfer this tacit knowledge?
options: Expand the written documentation to include more detailed procedures and checklists
Arrange for the operations team to shadow the project team during actual production runs before formal handover
Provide a video recording of the project team operating the equipment
Deliver a classroom training session using slide presentations
explanation: Tacit knowledge—experience, intuition, and nuanced skills that are difficult to articulate—is best transferred through direct observation, apprenticeship, and hands-on practice. Shadowing during actual production runs is the most effective method for this type of knowledge. Written documentation, videos, and classroom sessions are useful supplements but cannot fully capture tacit knowledge.
explanation_vi: Kiến thức ẩn—kinh nghiệm, trực giác và kỹ năng tinh tế khó diễn đạt—được chuyển giao hiệu quả nhất qua quan sát trực tiếp, học nghề và thực hành thực tế. Theo dõi trong các lần sản xuất thực tế là phương pháp hiệu quả nhất cho loại kiến thức này. Tài liệu viết, video và các buổi học trong lớp là bổ sung hữu ích nhưng không thể nắm bắt đầy đủ kiến thức ẩn.

question: An agile project has completed ten sprints. The product owner wants to ensure the organization benefits from the team's accumulated learning even if the project is cancelled or paused. What should the PM do to protect this knowledge?
options: Export all sprint retrospective notes and user stories to the organizational knowledge repository after each sprint
Keep all documentation within the team's private project folder as it belongs to the project
Wait for the project to reach a stable milestone before transferring any knowledge assets
Brief the portfolio manager verbally each month about team learnings
explanation: PMI best practice is to continuously update the organizational knowledge repository so that institutional knowledge is preserved regardless of what happens to the project. Exporting retrospective notes and artifacts after each sprint ensures that if the project is cancelled or paused, the organization retains the value of accumulated learning. Keeping knowledge in private project folders creates silos and risks loss.
explanation_vi: Thực hành tốt nhất của PMI là liên tục cập nhật kho kiến thức của tổ chức để bảo tồn kiến thức thể chế bất kể điều gì xảy ra với dự án. Xuất ghi chú hồi cố và các hiện vật sau mỗi sprint đảm bảo rằng nếu dự án bị hủy hoặc tạm dừng, tổ chức vẫn giữ được giá trị của kiến thức tích lũy.

question: A PM is planning a complex IT migration project that will last 18 months. She is concerned about knowledge retention as the team may change over time. During planning, what should she include in the project management plan to address this risk proactively?
options: A knowledge management plan that includes scheduled lessons learned sessions, a wiki for decisions, and cross-training requirements
A staffing management plan that prohibits team member changes for the project duration
A risk response to replace departing members within two weeks to minimize disruption
A communication plan that includes monthly status emails summarizing project progress
explanation: Proactively planning for knowledge management—scheduled lessons learned sessions, a centralized wiki, and cross-training requirements—directly addresses the risk of knowledge loss due to team changes. Prohibiting staff changes is unrealistic. Rapid replacement alone does not transfer knowledge. Monthly status emails address communication but not knowledge retention.
explanation_vi: Lập kế hoạch chủ động cho quản lý kiến thức—các buổi bài học kinh nghiệm định kỳ, wiki tập trung và yêu cầu đào tạo chéo—trực tiếp giải quyết rủi ro mất kiến thức do thay đổi nhóm. Cấm thay đổi nhân sự là không thực tế. Thay thế nhanh một mình không chuyển giao kiến thức.

question: A project manager is closing Phase 2 of a large program and wants to ensure comprehensive knowledge transfer to the Phase 3 team. Which TWO actions best support this goal?
options: Conduct structured knowledge transfer sessions with overlapping team membership between phases
Archive all Phase 2 documents in a shared repository with clear labeling and search tags
Verbally brief the Phase 3 team lead in a single meeting and provide no further support
Delete Phase 2 working files after archiving the final deliverables to save storage space
explanation: Structured overlap sessions allow tacit knowledge to be shared directly between outgoing and incoming team members. Archiving documents with clear labeling ensures explicit knowledge is findable and reusable. A single verbal briefing is insufficient, and deleting working files removes potentially valuable context about how decisions were made.
explanation_vi: Các buổi chuyển tiếp có cấu trúc với thành viên nhóm chồng chéo cho phép kiến thức ẩn được chia sẻ trực tiếp giữa các thành viên nhóm sắp ra và sắp vào. Lưu trữ tài liệu với nhãn rõ ràng đảm bảo kiến thức hiện có thể tìm thấy và tái sử dụng.

question: The project team has completed development of a new business intelligence platform. As the PM prepares the transition to the BAU operations team, he finds that the operations team has different tools, vocabulary, and processes than the project team. What should the PM do to bridge this gap?
options: Require the operations team to adopt the project team's vocabulary and tools as a condition of handover
Develop a transition plan that translates project artifacts into operations-friendly formats, including glossaries and role-specific guides
Hand over all project documentation unchanged and let the operations team interpret it themselves
Escalate to senior leadership to mandate alignment between project and operations teams before transition
explanation: Effective transition planning requires tailoring handoff materials to the receiving team's context—their tools, vocabulary, and processes. Developing operations-friendly documentation with glossaries and role-specific guides ensures the operations team can actually use the knowledge transferred. Requiring the operations team to adopt project vocabulary shifts the burden inappropriately. Handing over unchanged documents risks misinterpretation.
explanation_vi: Lập kế hoạch chuyển giao hiệu quả đòi hỏi điều chỉnh tài liệu bàn giao theo bối cảnh của nhóm tiếp nhận—công cụ, từ vựng và quy trình của họ. Phát triển tài liệu thân thiện với vận hành kèm bảng thuật ngữ và hướng dẫn theo vai trò đảm bảo nhóm vận hành thực sự có thể sử dụng kiến thức được chuyển giao.

question: A product owner in an agile team notices that the team consistently forgets solutions to recurring technical problems because retrospective notes are not systematically stored. Team velocity suffers as the same issues are rediscovered each sprint. What should the PM recommend?
options: Increase sprint length to give the team more time to research recurring issues
Establish a searchable team wiki where solutions to recurring problems are documented and maintained after each sprint
Ask the senior developer to memorize common solutions and brief the team at stand-up
Add recurring technical problems to the risk register and revisit them at project closure
explanation: A searchable team wiki is the appropriate knowledge management tool for capturing reusable solutions, reducing rediscovery time, and improving velocity. PMI and agile frameworks both emphasize continuous learning and knowledge reuse. Increasing sprint length does not address the root cause. Relying on one person's memory creates a single point of failure. Adding to the risk register without acting on the knowledge does not resolve the issue.
explanation_vi: Wiki nhóm có khả năng tìm kiếm là công cụ quản lý kiến thức phù hợp để ghi lại các giải pháp có thể tái sử dụng, giảm thời gian tái khám phá và cải thiện vận tốc. PMI và các framework agile đều nhấn mạnh học hỏi liên tục và tái sử dụng kiến thức.

question: At the end of a government construction project, the PM is preparing the lessons learned register. A team member suggests that lessons learned should only include things that went wrong. The PM disagrees. What is the correct PMI perspective?
options: Lessons learned should only capture failures to ensure future teams avoid the same mistakes
Lessons learned should capture both successes and failures, including what worked well and what should be changed
Lessons learned are optional on government projects if the client does not require them
Lessons learned should be captured only by the PM, not team members, to ensure objectivity
explanation: PMI's PMBOK defines lessons learned as information gained during a project about what worked well and what did not, including both positive and negative experiences. Capturing only failures misses valuable insights about effective practices that should be replicated. Lessons learned are a standard knowledge management output, not optional. All team members should contribute to ensure comprehensive and diverse perspectives.
explanation_vi: PMBOK của PMI định nghĩa bài học kinh nghiệm là thông tin thu được trong dự án về những gì đã hoạt động tốt và những gì không, bao gồm cả kinh nghiệm tích cực và tiêu cực. Chỉ ghi lại thất bại bỏ lỡ những hiểu biết có giá trị về các thực hành hiệu quả cần được nhân rộng.

question: A PM leads a hybrid project where developers work in two-week sprints but the overall project follows a traditional waterfall lifecycle with quarterly steering committee reviews. Halfway through the project, the steering committee asks why there is no formal lessons learned register despite six months of work. What should the PM do going forward?
options: Explain that agile teams don't use lessons learned registers and propose replacing it with a retrospective summary
Synthesize retrospective outputs from each sprint into a formal lessons learned register entry and commit to updating it regularly
Delay creation of the lessons learned register until the final phase of the project
Delegate the lessons learned register entirely to the scrum master to manage
explanation: In a hybrid environment, the PM must bridge agile and traditional governance requirements. Retrospective outputs are the agile equivalent of lessons learned and should be synthesized into the formal register to meet steering committee expectations. Delaying until the final phase contradicts PMI's continuous capture principle. Delegation alone without oversight is insufficient for governance compliance.
explanation_vi: Trong môi trường kết hợp, PM phải kết nối các yêu cầu quản trị agile và truyền thống. Kết quả hồi cố là tương đương agile của bài học kinh nghiệm và cần được tổng hợp vào sổ chính thức để đáp ứng kỳ vọng của ủy ban chỉ đạo. Trì hoãn đến giai đoạn cuối mâu thuẫn với nguyên tắc thu thập liên tục của PMI.

question: A large IT project is near completion. The PM wants to ensure that the team's institutional knowledge does not leave with team members as they are released back to their functional departments. What is the BEST tool or technique to prevent this knowledge loss?
options: Conduct exit interviews with each departing team member to capture their individual knowledge
Ask team members to write personal development plans documenting what they learned
Implement cross-training sessions so multiple team members understand each critical area, and document all key decisions and technical rationale in the lessons learned register
Require all team members to remain available by phone for six months after the project ends
explanation: Cross-training reduces bus-factor risk by ensuring multiple team members share critical knowledge, while documenting key decisions and rationale in the lessons learned register preserves institutional memory for future projects. Exit interviews and personal development plans are useful but less systematic. Requiring extended availability is impractical and doesn't systematically transfer knowledge.
explanation_vi: Đào tạo chéo giảm rủi ro bus-factor bằng cách đảm bảo nhiều thành viên nhóm chia sẻ kiến thức quan trọng, trong khi ghi lại các quyết định chính và lý do trong sổ bài học kinh nghiệm bảo tồn ký ức tổ chức cho các dự án tương lai.

question: A project manager is creating the handoff package for a newly built customer portal being transferred to the IT operations team. The operations team manager says they only need the technical specifications. The PM believes more is needed. What additional elements should the PM insist on including?
options: Only the source code repository link, as the operations team can find all information there
Operational runbooks, known issues log, escalation contacts, maintenance schedules, and a FAQ for common operational scenarios
The original project charter and stakeholder register to give the operations team full project context
A list of team members who worked on the project in case the operations team has questions
explanation: A comprehensive handoff package for operations should include runbooks (step-by-step operating procedures), known issues, escalation paths, and maintenance guidance—materials that enable day-to-day management of the system. Source code alone is insufficient for operators who need to run, not build, the system. The project charter and stakeholder register are project governance documents, not operational tools.
explanation_vi: Gói bàn giao toàn diện cho vận hành cần bao gồm runbook (quy trình vận hành từng bước), các vấn đề đã biết, đường dẫn leo thang và hướng dẫn bảo trì—các tài liệu giúp quản lý hệ thống hàng ngày. Mã nguồn đơn độc không đủ cho những người vận hành cần chạy, không xây dựng hệ thống.

question: An agile project is being dissolved after successful delivery. The PM wants to ensure the organization captures maximum value from the project experience. Which TWO actions best fulfill the PMI knowledge management objective?
options: Archive all sprint retrospective notes, user stories, and technical decisions in the organizational knowledge repository
Conduct a final team retrospective focused on what practices should be adopted across the organization
Delete all project artifacts once the product is live to reduce data management overhead
Ask the team to submit time sheets for hours spent on knowledge management activities for billing purposes
explanation: Archiving project artifacts in the organizational knowledge repository ensures explicit knowledge is preserved and reusable. Conducting a final retrospective focused on organizational adoption captures insights that can improve delivery practices across teams. Deleting artifacts destroys institutional value. Time sheet submissions do not advance knowledge management.
explanation_vi: Lưu trữ các hiện vật dự án trong kho kiến thức tổ chức đảm bảo kiến thức hiện được bảo tồn và có thể tái sử dụng. Tiến hành hồi cố cuối cùng tập trung vào việc áp dụng tổ chức thu thập những hiểu biết có thể cải thiện các thực hành giao hàng trên nhiều nhóm.

question: During execution of a 30-month ERP implementation, the PM discovers that the lessons learned register has not been updated since project initiation eight months ago. The project management plan requires continuous updates. The project sponsor has asked for a status review next week and wants evidence that knowledge management is working. Meanwhile, two senior consultants are scheduled to roll off the project in two weeks. What is the PM's BEST course of action to address all of these pressures simultaneously?
options: Schedule a dedicated catch-up lessons learned session before the sponsor review, arrange knowledge transfer sessions with the rolling-off consultants, and establish a recurring lessons learned cadence for the remainder of the project
Inform the sponsor that lessons learned will be comprehensively captured at project closure as per industry practice
Ask the two departing consultants to document everything they know before they leave, and address the register gap at the next project milestone
Request a project pause from the sponsor until all knowledge management activities are current
explanation: This scenario requires juggling multiple pressures: governance accountability to the sponsor, imminent loss of tacit knowledge from departing consultants, and a systematic gap in the lessons learned process. The only option that addresses all three simultaneously is to run a catch-up session before the sponsor review, arrange knowledge transfer while the consultants are still available, and institute a recurring cadence going forward. Deferring to closure loses real-time value and exposes the project to knowledge loss. A sequential approach (address consultants then the register) is too slow given the two-week window. Pausing the project is disproportionate.
explanation_vi: Tình huống này đòi hỏi xử lý nhiều áp lực cùng lúc: trách nhiệm quản trị với nhà tài trợ, nguy cơ mất kiến thức ẩn từ các tư vấn sắp rời đi và khoảng cách hệ thống trong quy trình bài học kinh nghiệm. Lựa chọn duy nhất giải quyết cả ba đồng thời là tổ chức buổi bắt kịp trước cuộc đánh giá của nhà tài trợ, sắp xếp chuyển giao kiến thức trong khi các tư vấn vẫn còn đó và thiết lập nhịp độ định kỳ.

question: A software development project has reached its planned end date. The PM has obtained client sign-off on all deliverables and the system has gone live. However, contracts with two vendors are still open and final invoices have not been received. What should the PM do NEXT?
options: Declare the project closed since the client has accepted all deliverables and the system is live
Initiate contract closeout with both vendors by requesting final invoices, reconciling costs, and formally closing the contracts
Release all project team members immediately since their work is done
Transfer the open vendor contracts to the operations team to manage going forward
explanation: PMI's project closure process requires both administrative closure (deliverables accepted, team released, documents archived) and contract closeout (final invoices, payment, contract termination). Client acceptance alone does not constitute complete project closure. Open vendor contracts must be formally closed before the project can be declared complete. Releasing the team before contracts are closed may leave critical closeout tasks without owners.
explanation_vi: Quy trình đóng cửa dự án của PMI yêu cầu cả đóng cửa hành chính (các sản phẩm bàn giao được chấp nhận, nhóm được giải phóng, tài liệu được lưu trữ) và đóng cửa hợp đồng (hóa đơn cuối cùng, thanh toán, chấm dứt hợp đồng). Sự chấp nhận của khách hàng một mình không cấu thành việc đóng cửa dự án hoàn chỉnh.

question: A PM is closing a project that was cancelled by executive leadership due to a strategic pivot, even though only 40% of the work was completed. A team member argues that formal closure is unnecessary since the project never finished. What should the PM explain?
options: The team member is correct; formal closure only applies to successfully completed projects
Formal closure is still required for cancelled projects to release resources, close contracts, archive documents, and capture lessons learned
The project should be put on hold rather than closed, in case the strategic direction changes again
Only financial closure is required for a cancelled project; other closure activities can be skipped
explanation: PMI explicitly states that formal project closure is required even for cancelled or terminated projects. Closure activities must still be performed: resources must be released, contracts must be closed, documents must be archived, and lessons learned must be captured. Failing to perform closure for cancelled projects creates administrative, financial, and contractual risks for the organization.
explanation_vi: PMI nêu rõ rằng đóng cửa dự án chính thức là bắt buộc ngay cả đối với các dự án bị hủy hoặc chấm dứt. Các hoạt động đóng cửa vẫn phải được thực hiện: tài nguyên phải được giải phóng, hợp đồng phải được đóng, tài liệu phải được lưu trữ và bài học kinh nghiệm phải được ghi lại.

question: During project closure, the PM discovers that the actual project costs exceed the approved budget by 8% due to scope changes that were properly managed. The client has accepted all deliverables. What should the PM do regarding financial closure?
options: Close the project without addressing the cost overrun since the client accepted the deliverables
Perform cost reconciliation, document the variance with approved change records as justification, issue or process final invoices, and close financial accounts
Open a new project to cover the cost overrun as a separate financial matter
Absorb the cost overrun into the project contingency reserve without documentation
explanation: Financial closure requires reconciling all costs, documenting variances with appropriate justification (in this case, approved change orders), processing final invoices, and formally closing financial accounts. This creates an accurate financial record and demonstrates accountability. Simply ignoring the variance or treating it as a new project is incorrect. Absorbing it without documentation misrepresents the project's financial history.
explanation_vi: Đóng cửa tài chính đòi hỏi đối chiếu tất cả các chi phí, ghi lại các sai lệch với lý do phù hợp (trong trường hợp này là các lệnh thay đổi đã được phê duyệt), xử lý hóa đơn cuối cùng và chính thức đóng các tài khoản tài chính. Điều này tạo ra hồ sơ tài chính chính xác và thể hiện trách nhiệm giải trình.

question: An agile project has delivered its final product increment and the product owner has accepted the backlog as done. The team is eager to disperse to new projects. The PM wants to ensure proper closure before releasing the team. What PMI closure activity is most often skipped in agile environments that the PM should prioritize?
options: Conducting a final sprint review to demonstrate the product to stakeholders
Formally archiving project artifacts, capturing final lessons learned, and obtaining written stakeholder acceptance
Updating the team velocity chart for the last sprint
Deleting the sprint boards and backlog items to free up tool licenses
explanation: In agile environments, teams often rely on the product owner's verbal acceptance and informal practices, but PMI requires formal written acceptance, archival of project artifacts, and a final lessons learned capture as part of proper closure—regardless of methodology. These activities protect the organization and provide evidence of completion. Velocity charts and tool cleanup are minor administrative matters, not closure requirements.
explanation_vi: Trong môi trường agile, các nhóm thường dựa vào sự chấp nhận bằng miệng của chủ sở hữu sản phẩm và các thực hành không chính thức, nhưng PMI yêu cầu sự chấp nhận bằng văn bản chính thức, lưu trữ các hiện vật dự án và ghi lại bài học kinh nghiệm cuối cùng như một phần của việc đóng cửa đúng cách—bất kể phương pháp luận.

question: A PM is preparing to close a two-year construction project. The project sponsor asks the PM to skip the team recognition event to save budget in the final weeks. What should the PM recommend?
options: Agree with the sponsor since budget savings at closure are always a priority
Recommend proceeding with a recognition event, even if scaled back, because recognizing team contributions is a PMI closure best practice that supports morale and organizational culture
Conduct a virtual recognition email instead and skip any event entirely to save the maximum budget
Defer recognition until after the project is administratively closed and funded by a separate HR budget
explanation: PMI's project closure best practices include team recognition and celebration as a formal activity that acknowledges contributions, supports organizational culture, and positively affects morale for future projects. A scaled-back event balances cost constraints with this important practice. Skipping recognition entirely, especially after a two-year project, can damage team morale and organizational reputation. A simple email does not fulfill the spirit of recognition.
explanation_vi: Thực hành tốt nhất về đóng cửa dự án của PMI bao gồm ghi nhận và ăn mừng nhóm như một hoạt động chính thức thừa nhận đóng góp, hỗ trợ văn hóa tổ chức và ảnh hưởng tích cực đến tinh thần cho các dự án tương lai. Một sự kiện thu nhỏ cân bằng giữa hạn chế chi phí với thực hành quan trọng này.

question: During project closure, the PM is reviewing whether all closure criteria have been met. She has confirmed deliverables are accepted and documents are archived. She still needs to confirm two more items before declaring the project closed. Which of the following are valid remaining closure criteria she should verify?
options: Verify that the project won an industry award and that the team was promoted
Confirm that all contracts are formally closed and all project resources have been formally released
Confirm that the project schedule baseline was never changed and that no risks occurred
Verify that the project plan was approved by the board at initiation and that all WBS elements were executed
explanation: The standard PMI closure criteria include: deliverables accepted, contracts closed, resources released, and documents archived. The PM has confirmed deliverables and archiving; the remaining items to verify are contract closure and formal resource release. Award recognition and promotions are not closure criteria. Schedule baseline changes and risks are not closure criteria. WBS completion is related to scope verification, which should have been confirmed during deliverable acceptance.
explanation_vi: Tiêu chí đóng cửa PMI tiêu chuẩn bao gồm: các sản phẩm bàn giao được chấp nhận, hợp đồng đóng, tài nguyên được giải phóng và tài liệu được lưu trữ. PM đã xác nhận các sản phẩm bàn giao và lưu trữ; các mục còn lại cần xác minh là đóng cửa hợp đồng và giải phóng tài nguyên chính thức.

question: A PM is closing the final phase of a multi-year program. A post-implementation review was scheduled three months after go-live to assess whether the expected business benefits have been realized. The PM is about to close the project administratively. Who is responsible for the post-implementation review and what should the PM do?
options: The PM should postpone administrative closure until after the post-implementation review is complete
The PM should complete administrative closure now and ensure the post-implementation review is formally handed off to the sponsoring business unit with clear ownership and timeline
The PM should cancel the post-implementation review since the project is complete and benefits realization is not a project management responsibility
The PM should assign one team member to remain on the project part-time to conduct the post-implementation review before being released
explanation: Administrative closure should not be delayed indefinitely pending benefits realization, which often occurs months after delivery. The correct approach is to close the project and formally hand off ownership of the post-implementation review to the sponsoring business unit or a program management office. This ensures accountability for benefits realization while allowing the project to close properly. Cancelling the review ignores benefits management obligations.
explanation_vi: Đóng cửa hành chính không nên bị trì hoãn vô thời hạn chờ thực hiện lợi ích, thường xảy ra nhiều tháng sau khi giao hàng. Cách tiếp cận đúng là đóng cửa dự án và chính thức bàn giao quyền sở hữu đánh giá sau triển khai cho đơn vị kinh doanh tài trợ hoặc văn phòng quản lý chương trình.

question: A project manager is closing a project where a major vendor provided software under a fixed-price contract. The vendor has fulfilled all contractual obligations and the deliverables have been accepted. What is the CORRECT process for closing this vendor contract?
options: Stop paying invoices and consider the contract closed once the deliverables are accepted
Send a formal written notice of contract closure to the vendor, confirm all deliverables are accepted, process the final payment, and archive the contract documents
Verbally thank the vendor for their work and allow the contract to expire naturally
Transfer the contract to the operations team for ongoing management
explanation: Contract closeout requires a formal written notice to the vendor confirming that all deliverables have been accepted and the contract is closed, processing the final payment, and archiving all contract documentation. PMI's procurement closure process ensures legal and financial obligations are met and provides a clear record. Verbal thanks or allowing expiration do not constitute formal closure. An operations contract would be separate from the project contract.
explanation_vi: Đóng cửa hợp đồng đòi hỏi thông báo bằng văn bản chính thức cho nhà cung cấp xác nhận rằng tất cả các sản phẩm bàn giao đã được chấp nhận và hợp đồng đã đóng, xử lý khoản thanh toán cuối cùng và lưu trữ tất cả tài liệu hợp đồng. Quy trình đóng cửa mua sắm của PMI đảm bảo các nghĩa vụ pháp lý và tài chính được đáp ứng.

question: An agile team has just completed the final sprint of a product development project. The product owner has accepted the last increment. The team wants to immediately start on a new project. What should the PM ensure is done before the team disperses?
options: Nothing—in agile, product owner acceptance of the last increment is sufficient to close the project
Conduct a final retrospective, capture lessons learned, obtain formal written stakeholder acceptance, archive artifacts, release resources formally, and update the organizational knowledge repository
Run one additional sprint to polish any remaining items before closure
Ask the product owner to write a brief summary email as the official closure document
explanation: Even in agile projects, PMI requires formal closure activities: a final retrospective, lessons learned capture, written stakeholder acceptance, document archival, formal resource release, and knowledge repository update. These ensure the organization retains value from the project experience and provides clear evidence of project completion. Product owner acceptance of an increment is not the same as formal project closure.
explanation_vi: Ngay cả trong các dự án agile, PMI yêu cầu các hoạt động đóng cửa chính thức: hồi cố cuối cùng, thu thập bài học kinh nghiệm, sự chấp nhận bằng văn bản của các bên liên quan, lưu trữ tài liệu, giải phóng tài nguyên chính thức và cập nhật kho kiến thức tổ chức.

question: A multi-phase project uses phase-gate reviews to govern transitions between phases. The project has just completed Phase 2 and must pass through a phase-gate before Phase 3 can begin. What should the PM prepare to demonstrate successful phase closure?
options: A verbal briefing to the steering committee summarizing Phase 2 highlights
Formal phase closure documentation including deliverable acceptance records, updated lessons learned register, phase performance summary, and resource release plan for Phase 3 transition
The original Phase 2 project plan unchanged, showing that all tasks are marked complete
An updated project budget showing Phase 2 spending to date
explanation: Phase-gate closure requires formal documentation that demonstrates the phase is complete and the project is ready to proceed. This includes deliverable acceptance records, an updated lessons learned register, a phase performance summary (schedule, cost, scope), and the resource transition plan. A verbal briefing lacks documentation rigor. The original plan does not reflect outcomes. Budget data alone is insufficient for phase-gate governance.
explanation_vi: Đóng cửa phase-gate yêu cầu tài liệu chính thức chứng minh giai đoạn đã hoàn thành và dự án sẵn sàng tiến hành. Điều này bao gồm hồ sơ chấp nhận sản phẩm bàn giao, sổ bài học kinh nghiệm đã cập nhật, tóm tắt hiệu suất giai đoạn và kế hoạch chuyển giao tài nguyên.

question: At project closure, the PM needs to formally release project resources back to their functional departments. Two team members have expressed interest in continuing to support the newly delivered system. What is the CORRECT approach for the PM?
options: Allow the two interested team members to continue on the project indefinitely without formal release
Formally release all resources to their functional managers, and if support is needed, coordinate with functional managers for any formal arrangement through operational support channels
Keep all team members on the project for six months as a buffer in case issues arise post-delivery
Release the two interested members last, after all other team members have been released
explanation: Formal resource release is a required closure activity. All resources must be released back to functional managers (or resource pools) as part of project closure. Any ongoing support needs should be addressed through operational channels, not by keeping people indefinitely on the project. Releasing team members in different sequences based on personal interest is not a governance-compliant practice.
explanation_vi: Giải phóng tài nguyên chính thức là hoạt động đóng cửa bắt buộc. Tất cả tài nguyên phải được trả lại cho các nhà quản lý chức năng (hoặc nhóm tài nguyên) như một phần của việc đóng cửa dự án. Mọi nhu cầu hỗ trợ liên tục cần được giải quyết thông qua các kênh vận hành, không phải bằng cách giữ người vô thời hạn trong dự án.

question: A PM is six months into closing a large enterprise transformation project. The client's key decision-maker who originally signed the project charter has retired, and a new executive has taken over. The new executive is reluctant to sign the formal project acceptance documents because she was not involved in the project. What should the PM do?
options: Close the project without formal sign-off since the original sponsor is no longer available
Work with the client organization to identify the proper authority to provide formal acceptance and obtain written sign-off before proceeding with final closure
Ask a junior client contact to sign the acceptance documents to keep the project moving
Accept verbal confirmation from the new executive as equivalent to written sign-off
explanation: Formal written stakeholder acceptance is a required closure activity. When the original decision-maker is unavailable, the PM must work with the client organization to identify and engage the appropriate authority with decision-making power. Closing without sign-off creates legal and contractual risk. A junior contact lacks authority. Verbal confirmation is not a substitute for formal written acceptance in project governance.
explanation_vi: Sự chấp nhận bằng văn bản chính thức của các bên liên quan là hoạt động đóng cửa bắt buộc. Khi người ra quyết định ban đầu không còn, PM phải làm việc với tổ chức khách hàng để xác định và thu hút người có thẩm quyền thích hợp với quyền ra quyết định.

question: A project manager has been asked to close a project that was terminated early due to budget cuts. The team is discouraged, feeling that their work was wasted. How should the PM handle the closure of this project?
options: Skip the team recognition step since there are no deliverables to celebrate

Conduct a proper closure including acknowledging team effort, capturing lessons learned from the work completed, archiving documentation, releasing resources formally, and recognizing individual contributions
Focus only on financial closure and contract closeout since they are the most legally significant
Close the project as quickly as possible to minimize further cost and avoid discussing the cancellation
explanation: Even for terminated projects, PMI requires a full closure process including team recognition for the work performed, lessons learned, archiving, and resource release. Recognizing team contributions—even on a cancelled project—maintains morale and demonstrates respect for effort. Lessons learned from a cancelled project are especially valuable for understanding what to avoid in future projects. Skipping recognition is contrary to PMI values and harmful to organizational culture.
explanation_vi: Ngay cả đối với các dự án bị chấm dứt, PMI yêu cầu quy trình đóng cửa đầy đủ bao gồm ghi nhận nhóm cho công việc đã thực hiện, bài học kinh nghiệm, lưu trữ và giải phóng tài nguyên. Ghi nhận đóng góp của nhóm—ngay cả trên một dự án bị hủy—duy trì tinh thần và thể hiện sự tôn trọng nỗ lực.

question: An agile project has been running for 14 months and is now closing. The PM realizes that the benefits the project was supposed to deliver—a 20% reduction in customer churn—cannot yet be measured because the product has only been live for three weeks. What should the PM do regarding benefits realization?
options: Claim the benefits as realized in the closure report since the product is now live
Document the expected benefits, establish measurement criteria, and transfer responsibility for tracking benefits realization to the product owner or sponsoring business unit, then close the project
Delay project closure until the 20% churn reduction has been confirmed
Remove the benefit target from the project closure report since it cannot be confirmed yet
explanation: Benefits realization often cannot be confirmed at project closure because it takes time after delivery to observe outcomes. The correct approach is to document expected benefits, establish measurement criteria and timelines, and transfer ownership of benefits tracking to the business. The project can then be formally closed. Claiming benefits not yet achieved is dishonest. Delaying closure indefinitely is impractical. Removing benefit targets misrepresents the project's purpose.
explanation_vi: Việc thực hiện lợi ích thường không thể được xác nhận khi đóng cửa dự án vì cần có thời gian sau khi giao hàng để quan sát kết quả. Cách tiếp cận đúng là ghi lại các lợi ích kỳ vọng, thiết lập tiêu chí đo lường và thời gian, và chuyển quyền sở hữu theo dõi lợi ích cho doanh nghiệp.

question: A PM is preparing a project closure checklist for a major infrastructure project. Which TWO items from the following list are required components of a complete project closure?
options: Formal written acceptance of all deliverables by the authorized client representative
Confirmation that the project delivered at least one additional scope item beyond what was contracted
Archival of all project documents in the organizational knowledge repository
Proof that the project team exceeded their personal performance targets for the year
explanation: PMI's closure criteria include formal written deliverable acceptance by an authorized stakeholder and archival of project documentation in the organizational knowledge repository. These ensure legal protection, provide a record of completion, and preserve institutional knowledge. Delivering additional scope beyond what was contracted is not a closure requirement (and may indicate scope creep). Personal performance targets are HR matters unrelated to project closure.
explanation_vi: Tiêu chí đóng cửa của PMI bao gồm sự chấp nhận bằng văn bản chính thức về các sản phẩm bàn giao của các bên liên quan có thẩm quyền và lưu trữ tài liệu dự án trong kho kiến thức tổ chức. Điều này đảm bảo bảo vệ pháp lý, cung cấp hồ sơ hoàn thành và bảo tồn kiến thức thể chế.

question: A PM is closing a project where the procurement team managed three vendor contracts. Two contracts have been formally closed, but the third vendor has disputed the final invoice, claiming additional work was performed outside the original scope. The PM believes the claim is not supported by the contract. What should the PM do?
options: Pay the disputed invoice to speed up contract closure and move on
Work with the procurement team and legal counsel to review the contract terms and dispute resolution process before closing the contract
Close all other project activities and leave the vendor dispute open for the operations team to resolve
Refuse all communication with the vendor and declare the contract closed unilaterally
explanation: Contract disputes must be resolved through the formal dispute resolution process defined in the contract, with involvement of procurement and legal teams. PMI emphasizes that contracts cannot be closed until all claims are resolved. Paying an unsupported claim wastes organizational funds. Leaving disputes for operations transfers a project responsibility inappropriately. Unilateral closure without resolution creates legal exposure.
explanation_vi: Các tranh chấp hợp đồng phải được giải quyết thông qua quy trình giải quyết tranh chấp chính thức được xác định trong hợp đồng, với sự tham gia của các nhóm mua sắm và pháp lý. PMI nhấn mạnh rằng các hợp đồng không thể được đóng cho đến khi tất cả các khiếu nại được giải quyết.

question: A PM is about to close a project that used an agile delivery model but was governed by a traditional contract. The client organization's PMO requires a formal project closure report, but the PM has only retrospective notes and sprint artifacts. What should the PM do?
options: Submit the sprint artifacts as-is and inform the PMO that a formal report is not compatible with agile
Synthesize the sprint retrospectives, velocity data, acceptance records, and final backlog status into a formal closure report that meets PMO requirements
Create a new project to write the closure report since it was not part of the original project scope
Ask the PMO to waive the closure report requirement since the project was delivered successfully
explanation: In a hybrid context, the PM must translate agile artifacts into the governance outputs required by the organization. Sprint retrospectives, velocity data, acceptance records, and backlog status can all be synthesized into a formal closure report that satisfies PMO requirements while reflecting the agile delivery approach. Refusing governance requirements is not acceptable. Creating a new project for the report is unnecessary. Requesting a waiver is inappropriate when the data exists to fulfill the requirement.
explanation_vi: Trong bối cảnh kết hợp, PM phải dịch các hiện vật agile thành các đầu ra quản trị mà tổ chức yêu cầu. Kết quả hồi cố sprint, dữ liệu vận tốc, hồ sơ chấp nhận và trạng thái backlog đều có thể được tổng hợp thành báo cáo đóng cửa chính thức đáp ứng yêu cầu PMO trong khi phản ánh cách tiếp cận giao hàng agile.
